title: Souls and Rain-Drops.
content:
Light rain-drops fall and wrinkle the sea,
    Then vanish, and die utterly.
    One would not know that rain-drops fell
    If the round sea-wrinkles did not tell.

    So souls come down and wrinkle life
    And vanish in the flesh-sea strife.
    One might not know that souls had place
    Were't not for the wrinkles in life's face.
meaning:
Sidney Lanier's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Souls and Rain-Drops."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
